summary:
The condition involving two X and one Y chromosome has a karyotype of 47,XXY, clinically identified as Klinefelter syndrome. This disorder presents with characteristics such as infertility, reduced testosterone levels, gynecomastia, and increased height due to abnormal sex chromosomal inheritance patterns affecting gonadal function.

Note: While some retrieval results did not name “Kleineftel” outright, their content consistently linked the specific genetic makeup—specifically XX+Y—to classic symptoms seen exclusively within this diagnosis.

---

---. Answer choice: A
